channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don’t like Meta’s changes
item.description: Some users are wondering how to quit the platforms now that Meta is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its fact checking program and replacing it with crowdsourced notes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: How an AI-generated ‘expert’ sank into media deadlines
item.description: Ashley Abramson first came across Sophie Cress in a cold pitch to her work email. Cress was asking to be an expert source for any stories Abramson was working on as a freelance reporter.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Planted envelope, pilfered identities, money that didn’t exist: Bay Area couple accused of $60 million AI startup fraud
item.description: The indictment describes an enterprise constantly teetering on the edge of failure as the Beckmans allegedly solicited and obtained investments via false financial documents that dramatically overstated company sales, profit and bank balances.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Bun 1.2 squashes Node.js compatibility bugs
item.description: Bun 1.2, an update to the all-in-one runtime and toolkit for building, running, and testing full-stack JavaScript and TypeScript applications, has arrived, boasting dramatic improvements in Node.js compatibility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Modernizing bp’s application landscape with AI
item.description: One of the oldest and largest oil and gas companies in the world, bp is in the midst of a major transition as it pivots toward becoming an integrated energy company. As part of this transition, the company is aiming for a net-zero carbon footprint by 2050.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How dangerous are 3D printers? Maybe enough for a background check
item.description: As 3D-printed gun violence abounds, some lawmakers are looking to cut the problem at the root. The New York state senate is currently evaluating a bill that would dramatically change the 3D printing market.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Meta nearly doubling spending with eye toward AI
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced Friday the social media company will nearly double its spending this year as it focuses on artificial intelligence (AI) development. Meta is planning to invest $60 billion to $65 billion in 2025, Zuckerberg said Friday in a post on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ubisoft reveals Assassin's Creed Shadows PC requirements – and just as I thought, you're safe with your RTX 4000 series GPU
item.description: Nvidia's new RTX 5000 GPU lineup promises to enhance gaming performance in multiple games, but PC requirements for Ubisoft's new title suggest an upgrade isn't urgently needed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The first Android 16 public beta is here – and it borrows a key iOS feature
item.description: The first beta version of Android 16 is now available for Pixel phones, and there's one big new feature.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A new Instagram feature might expose your embarrassing habits
item.description: Instagram Reels has added a new feature that shows you a feed of videos that your friends have liked. The bad news: It works both ways, meaning your friends can now see every video you’ve liked.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Warren sends Musk 30 ideas for DOGE to cut government spending
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) sent tech billionaire Elon Musk 30 ideas for how the newly formed Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) could cut government spending.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he will use energy emergency declaration to approve more power for AI
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that he will use his energy emergency declaration to approve power stations for artificial intelligence (AI) centers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: 3 hidden benefits of Dedicated Internet Access for enterprises
item.description: Reliable Internet connectivity is crucial for businesses to function efficiently and remain competitive in today’s digital world. While standard broadband connections may work for basic tasks, they often struggle to meet the demands of modern businesses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing crypto working group
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a working group on digital assets led by David Sacks, the White House czar for artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptocurrency.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Palo Alto Networks Unveils Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) Open API Framework
item.description: Palo Alto Networks unveils QRNG Open API framework for quantum security threats, enabling multi-vendor interoperability and simplifying QRNG integration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ivanka Trump calls out 'fake crypto coin' named after her
item.description: Ivanka Trump, President Trump’s eldest daughter, warned Thursday that a cryptocurrency token bearing her name is fake.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN
item.description: Lt. Gen. Paul Stanton, commander of JFHQ-DoDIN, said commanders can better manage risks and focus on defending the applications and data that matters the most. The post Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: 81% of firms back a Zero Trust approach to cyber defense
item.description: A recent survey of cybersecurity professionals indicates that 81% of organizations are turning to zero trust as the foundation of their cybersecurity strategies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FinOps automation: Raising the bar on lowering cloud costs
item.description: IT professionals have been striving to manage cloud costs effectively since the inception of cloud computing. Those efforts have evolved into FinOps, a business discipline and a set of best practices to optimize cloud spending.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Outlook for Microsoft 365 cheat sheet
item.description: There are countless ways to communicate electronically, including texting, social media, chat apps, team task managers, and videoconferencing software. Given the myriad ways you can get in touch with others, you may well think email is dead. Think again.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration will usher in a crypto-friendly administration, and with it, new state policies
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The bitcoin-friendly administration of President-elect Donald Trump and an expanding lobbying effort in statehouses could push states to become more open to crypto and lead public pension funds and treasuries to buy into it.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: From devops to CTO: 5 things to start doing now
item.description: I was promoted to CTO in my late twenties, and while it is common to see young CTOs leading startups these days, it was unusual in the ’90s. I was far less experienced back then, and still developing my business acumen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: TikTok goes dark as US ban takes effect
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, WFLA, NBC
